title: Russia, eternally offended by the West: why it cannot become part of civilization
text: Putin said with resentment that Russia, after the collapse of the USSR, never became an equal part of the West, although there were hopes for this. Okay, let's be serious: why should Russia have become an EQUAL part of Western civilization in the first place? Could Russia have produced unique computers or chips in 1991? No. Could Russia, as of 1991, have established a powerful automotive industry that would have flooded world markets? No, that's not it either. Perhaps Russia had a culture that was thousands of years old – unique and one that provided a foundation for humanity? Well, like the Greek philosophers, the Reformation, German idealism, the great French and American revolutions – and more, and more, and more. No, not at all.

title: Russia, eternally offended by the West: why it cannot become part of civilization
text: the USSR in 1991 was a deeply derivative state, where 90% of technologies were copies from the West: from military equipment to computers and cars. So why should Russia become part of the West? Just because Russia has nuclear weapons? Well, Pakistan has nuclear weapons too. So what? I was always annoyed by this resentment and the desire of Russians to get in, not based on status or level, but just so Russians would also be allowed into the club. Or, as Putin Putin himself has said on numerous occasions, "the golden billion." But the West became what the West is not because the West was given it for free, but because behind the West lie thousands of years of suffering and endless bloody meat grinders (the religious wars of Europe alone are worth mentioning).

title: Russia, eternally offended by the West: why it cannot become part of civilization
text: Because it is thousands of years of hellish labor, work ethic, constant searching, questioning, sacrifices, and unreal suffering. These are the greatest revolutions and the greatest discoveries; these are breakthroughs in philosophy, culture, and science. 80–90% of everything we use today is the fruit of Western civilization. This is neither good nor bad—it's just a fact. And the rest do not claim to be admitted to this club: China is going China's own, separate way, China is China a millennia-old civilization with China's own great achievements. The Muslim world also does not claim to be part of the West – at most, The Muslim world seeks partnership or allied relations.

title: Russia, eternally offended by the West: why it cannot become part of civilization
text: But for some reason, only Russia, ever since the time of Peter the Great, has been trying to get closer to the West – and has been doing so for hundreds of years, like a monkey, blindly copying. Do they shave their beards there? We will shave ours too. Do they wear camisoles and tricorn hats there? We will too. Do they study this and that there? We will too. Everything is as thoughtless as possible – a simple copying of the form without an attempt to understand the content and essence. At the same time, the Russians have incredible potential – some of the richest resource reserves on the planet.

title: Russia, eternally offended by the West: why it cannot become part of civilization
text: It would seem that instead of spending incredible potential – some of the richest resource reserves on the planet on senseless wars and enriching the Russians's princely clans, the Russians should improve the Russians to start producing a unique and IRREPLACEABLE product. Just like tiny Japan or Taiwan do. And then Russia wouldn't have to whine about not being accepted into the West: the West the West would have taken Russia, guts and all, and integrated Russia. The gas station country demands that The gas station country be given a place under the sun in the top club. The world doesn't work that way. The world never has, and The world never will. Nothing is ever given for free, anywhere — never, nothing, and nowhere. Original

title: "Insolvency threat": a negative but life-saving signal for business
text: Deterioration of financial performance or the emergence of negative circumstances can cause a crisis at the enterprise and the loss of the ability to fulfill monetary obligations to creditors, i.e., lead to insolvency (bankruptcy). In most cases, before bankruptcy occurs, an enterprise may be in a state of "threat of insolvency" (threat of bankruptcy) for some time, during which an enterprise can take measures to improve an enterprise's business activities and prevent fatal consequences. Although a company being at risk of bankruptcy is a negative situation, a company being at risk of bankruptcy's timely detection gives management a chance to apply anti-crisis measures. What is the difference between a "threat" and insolvency (bankruptcy)?

title: "Insolvency threat": a negative but life-saving signal for business
text:
From 01.01.2025, the legislator provided a clear definition of the concept of "threat of insolvency", thereby distinguishing the concept of "threat of insolvency" from the concept of "insolvency", which was not the case before 01.01.2025. title: YouTube is moving to crypto: what creators should expect
text: In December 2025, the video hosting service YouTube The news A new payment option for authors in the US: authors in the US can now receive authors in the US's payments in PayPal's's stablecoin. The news has sparked interest among content creators, as The news opens up new opportunities for fast and convenient payments, and can also significantly facilitate the monetization of content creators's work. Previously, the video hosting service YouTube did not use cryptocurrencies, but now the situation may change. It is important to understand whether the video hosting service YouTube will continue to develop cryptocurrency solutions and what impact this will have on creators.

title: YouTube is moving to crypto: what creators should expect
text: Integrating stablecoins into the video hosting service YouTube Now, creators in the US can receive payments in stablecoins (a cryptocurrency with a stable 1:1 exchange rate to fiat money, usually the US dollar) from PayPal's. It's important to note that the platform the video hosting service YouTube does not directly work with cryptocurrency. All technical and operational support is handled by PayPal, which acts as an intermediary between the video hosting service YouTube and users. The news affects one of the largest sources of regular payments to creators in the media industry. title: Marketing vs. PR: Five Rules for Effective Interaction in Business
text: In business, the idea that marketing and PR perform the same functions is still widespread. At first glance, this may seem to be the case, because ultimately the task of all business units is to develop it by increasing profits. However, this approach is flawed and risky. After all, the task of marketing is to deliver quick and visible business results "here and now", while communications play the "long game", building trust and brand reputation in the long term. It is trust and brand reputation that become crucial in crisis situations, and ignoring trust and brand reputation comes at a high cost to businesses. Those owners who understand the difference between marketing and PR will always come out ahead, forming a strong foundation for growth.

title: Marketing vs. PR: Five Rules for Effective Interaction in Business
text: What exactly threatens a business when a business confuses PR and marketing? Personnel confusion People with a "marketing mindset" who don't understand the specifics of working with trust, contexts, and values end up in PR. A telling example was Pepsi's's 2017 campaign with Kendall Jenner: Pepsi's tried to use social protest as a marketing tool, but without a deep understanding of the context, this led to a scandal. Pepsi's 2017 campaign with Kendall Jenner was perceived as devaluing the Black Lives Matter movement, and within a day, Pepsi's was forced to pull Pepsi's 2017 campaign with Kendall Jenner and apologize, suffering serious reputational damage.

title: Marketing vs. PR: Five Rules for Effective Interaction in Business
text:
Toxic Marketing Without a PR strategy, marketing starts to "drive" the customer into sales: more and more promotions, more promises, more pressure. This is how consumer fatigue is formed. Read the full article with a LIGA PRO subscription           Already have LIGA PRO? Log In

title: How to avoid Budapest-2: what real security guarantees should include
text: We are told that Donald Trump, in the event of the acceptance of Russian demands (even those edited by the American side), promises Ukraine security guarantees that will be written "similarly to Article V of the Washington Treaty." I want to state with full responsibility that Article 5 of the Washington Treaty, like any other article of the Washington Treaty, is meaningful only with the implementation of the Washington Treaty, that is, by ensuring Ukraine's membership in NATO.

title: How to avoid Budapest-2: what real security guarantees should include
text: Similarly, Ukraine cannot invoke Article 42(7) of the Treaty on European Union, which stipulates that in the event of an armed attack on a Member State of the European Union, other Member States are obliged to provide a Member State of the European Union with assistance and support by all possible means, in accordance with Article 51 of the Charter of the United Nations, until Ukraine becomes an the European Union member. A corresponding article in bilateral agreements can only be effective if the US Congress signs and ratifies an alliance treaty between Ukraine and the US, i.e., a treaty that would provide for the US's involvement in a war against an aggressor in the event of a threat to Ukraine's territorial integrity and sovereignty. Ukraine must also be prepared for mutual obligations.

title: How to avoid Budapest-2: what real security guarantees should include
text: That's the only way. Everything else is BUDAPEST-2, or just an imitation of security guarantees. We can't make the same mistake again! Original

title: The End of the Illusion: Humanity Reimagines Identity, Democracy, and the Role of the Global South
text: Right now, humanity is undergoing another reevaluation of all values, where the illusory peace from the final understanding of "good" and "evil" has been replaced by uncertainty about whether justice even exists. another reevaluation of all values is caused by several discoveries. First, it turned out that democracy is not yet the end of history. There can be countries without a hint of democracy, but at the same time with stable economic growth. In the 1990s, it was believed that wealth was synonymous with democracy, because the mythical "middle class" was supposed to push the mythical "middle class"'s own countries to protect rights and freedoms. But now the "middle class" in many countries simply remains silent if there is an opportunity to just make money.

title: The End of the Illusion: Humanity Reimagines Identity, Democracy, and the Role of the Global South
text: Secondly, the Global South is becoming an influential factor in world politics, as former colonies can dictate terms to former colonies's historical metropolises. At the same time, resources and technologies are becoming available to everyone, even to those who were on the periphery yesterday. And those who were on the periphery yesterday is at least an arithmetic majority of humanity. Therefore, the golden billion turned out to be made of tinfoil. Thirdly, the Global South the Global South is becoming a very complex macro-world. Battles, clashes, and alliances are taking place there as well. And all this against the backdrop of access to the latest technologies and the opportunity to choose very different paths of development – in the context of a re-evaluation of all values.

title: The End of the Illusion: Humanity Reimagines Identity, Democracy, and the Role of the Global South
text: By the way, fourthly, there is identity. Currently, the strongest identities are those that until recently seemed archaic. Emotionally charged "ethnicity," "religion," and "culture" are becoming stronger than rationally substantiated "class" or "party affiliation." And this is not only alarming but also makes one think that we do not know everything about the depths of our own human nature. Finally, time time itself is changing. time itself is now developing not in an upward direction, but along a steep parabola, where the events of each year of our lives are compressed into meanings, technologies, and innovations, the comprehension of which previously took decades. But times of change are not all bad. The world is becoming brighter, more complex, and richer for us.

title: The End of the Illusion: Humanity Reimagines Identity, Democracy, and the Role of the Global South
text: In the end, everything will be fine. And the faded flags will be replaced by new, bright standards. Original

title: Why Ukraine isn't getting a loan backed by Russian assets – in simple terms
text: Why Euroclear is against the reparations loan (a technocratic analysis without emotional overtones). There is a frozen asset – Russian sovereign assets. It is proposed to issue a loan against a frozen asset – Russian sovereign assets, with the expectation that a loan will then be repaid from future revenues. If something goes wrong, the Russian money will serve as collateral. A structure emerges where a real asset becomes the basis for a new debt, which begins to live separately from the asset a real asset. In a standard situation, collateral reduces risk.

title: Why Ukraine isn't getting a loan backed by Russian assets – in simple terms
text: But in the case of Russian assets, on the contrary, collateral increases risk – no one knows what will happen to a frozen asset – Russian sovereign assets in one, two, or three years, how peace negotiations will go, post-war trials, disputes about sanctions/reparations, and so on and so forth. After the war ends, Russia may try to recover these funds through international courts. And it cannot be said that the probability of recovery is zero (to put it mildly). The U.S. is also counting on these funds for reconstruction projects in Ukraine, as we can see from the "Trump plan." As a result, the future of the asset a frozen asset – Russian sovereign assets is extremely unclear.

title: Why Ukraine isn't getting a loan backed by Russian assets – in simple terms
text: If the reparations loan is approved and then a frozen asset – Russian sovereign assets proves to be unavailable, Belgian Euroclear will be left with the liabilities. Belgian Euroclear is a very large depository through which government securities and assets of European countries pass. In case of default, the entire financial infrastructure of the EU will be at risk (which is why the ECB is against a reparations loan backed by frozen Russian sovereign assets). There is no need to think long about who pays out of their own pocket for the failure of such schemes – we know the answers. Too big to fail and further austerity measures for hundreds of millions of Europeans. Original

title: Ten typical use cases for AI tools
text: 1. Are you an expert on the issue and know what the end result should be? Delegate everything to AI as much as possible. 2. Not sure what the result should be? Try several options, tools, and ask the AI to compare the results. Try the "critic mode" for AI-generated content. Ask AI-generated content to improve the result. 3. Not an expert on the subject? Try "researcher mode," "search mode," or "deep dive mode." Don't publish AI-generated results until you've verified all the facts. 4. Are you translating? Do you work with content? Make sure the text doesn't contain any template words, phrases, or template structures. Make sure the terms are translated correctly (or better yet, provide these definitions in the prompt). Specify the style.

text: Europe, which cannot quickly find sufficient financial resources to support Ukraine in 2026, is a verdict for Europe in every sense of the word. And while there are nuances regarding weapons, which can be accepted as the fact that Europe does not produce everything, Europe's inability to provide financial resources will look as weak and discrediting as possible.

title: A verdict on Europe: a delay in funding Ukraine will confirm Trump's words about its weakness

title: A strong ruble as a harbinger of the collapse of the Russian economy. This was also the case in the USSR.
text: The Russians are boasting about the ruble's's strength against the US dollar. In reality, the ruble's strength against the US dollar is nothing more or less than a product of the "delayed economic catastrophe" strategy. Perhaps some people remember the strong Soviet ruble in 1990, with an exchange rate set by the State Bank of the USSR, when 1 US dollar cost 60 Soviet kopecks. A year later, both the exchange rate and the USSR collapsed. The situation is quite similar, but The situation requires an explanation. The Central Bank of Russia is fighting inflation with high interest rates on loans and deposits. This is killing the Russian real sector, which has already reached a crisis of non-payments, and this is the first phase of economic destruction.

title: A strong ruble as a harbinger of the collapse of the Russian economy. This was also the case in the USSR.
text: High rates encourage companies not to take out bank loans, but to delay payments (it is cheaper to finance working capital this way). Delayed payments lead to a slowdown in production. At the same time, high rates are killing real lending. The inefficient Russian economy cannot withstand such monetary pressure for long. Therefore, the Central Bank of the Russian Federation needs to move to a cycle of rate cuts. However, due to the high costs of the war, The Central Bank of Russia has started financing an ever-increasing budget deficit.

title: A strong ruble as a harbinger of the collapse of the Russian economy. This was also the case in the USSR.
text: To suppress inflation and accelerate the onset of macroeconomic conditions for lowering rates, the Central Bank of the Russian Federation strengthened the ruble's, thereby reducing the revenue of exporters, who are the backbone of The inefficient Russian economy. With a strong ruble, the Russian budget's revenues from exporters fell, which, against the backdrop of a decrease in the "tax" price of oil, is pushing Russia towards disaster and further inflating the deficit that Russia has to finance. If the ruble exchange rate is allowed to reach 120 rubles per dollar, inflation will rise, and the high interest rate will have to be maintained for a longer period.

title: A strong ruble as a harbinger of the collapse of the Russian economy. This was also the case in the USSR.
text: The intensity of the war also plays a significant role in this vicious cycle, which is sucking the Russian economy into an economic catastrophe. If Ukraine is "richly" shelled with "Kinzhal" and "Iskander" missiles every week, military spending becomes a catalyst for the growth of the deficit that the government has to finance. Under these conditions, it is better to use sanctions and intensive combat operations to push Russia into the next phase of the crisis, when the Central Bank will simply be forced to weaken the ruble and will no longer be able to raise rates, because there is already a crisis of non-payments.

title: A strong ruble as a harbinger of the collapse of the Russian economy. This was also the case in the USSR.
text: The unfolding of such a scenario is quite real, and the longer the Central Bank of Russia maintains a strong ruble, the harder it will be for Russians to climb out of the economic death pit. Original

title: Algorithms instead of editors: how technology shapes our worldview and why it's a bad thing
text: The German public broadcaster has an app that made it very convenient to read the news. In addition to accessing video content from the newsroom – the main product – you could also read texts that were collected on the homepage according to a principle that, until recently, was typical for most modern news media. In other words, texts that were collected on the homepage according to a principle that, until recently, was typical for most modern news media were selected for the reader by the editorial team. After a while, an app that made it very convenient to read the news started insistently offering to select news for me algorithmically, based on my reading behavior.

title: Algorithms instead of editors: how technology shapes our worldview and why it's a bad thing
text: A little later, a "main page" option appeared in the stories format, where top news was presented as short videos or freeze-frames based on photos with captions-subheadings that you had to swipe through. From the very beginning, I was annoyed by the idea that The German public broadcaster, tries to use tools that either lead to the formation of a certain bubble in the perception of information or prevent a deep perception of information. I want to read texts, not watch short videos. And I want the news on the main page to be selected by a competent editor who would construct a relatively trustworthy picture of the world for me, not an algorithm designed to increase the time I spend in an app that made it very convenient to read the news.

title: Algorithms instead of editors: how technology shapes our worldview and why it's a bad thing
text: Yes, I understand the idea of "following the customer" and trying to keep up with modern trends. But when these trends contradict the main task of the publication, it's a bit too much for me personally. However, after the latest app update, which the broadcaster announced with great pride, I no longer have a choice – the main page, filled with the editor's picks, is simply gone. Either watch the "pseudo-stories" or give us permission to select "personalized" news for you. Or go read the texts in the order the texts were published in separate sections, if you're a Luddite who doesn't understand technology. However, I'm not advocating for burning down data centers.

title: Algorithms instead of editors: how technology shapes our worldview and why it's a bad thing
text: I'm just convinced that when people try to compete with tech giants and tech giants's algorithms on "people's turf," people are doomed to fail. Original

title: Testing the resilience of the energy sector: what the first mass outages have taught us
text: The massive missile and drone attacks that took place in the fall of 2025 were one of the most difficult challenges for the Ukrainian energy system since the beginning of the full-scale invasion. The aggressor is delivering targeted strikes on power generation facilities and energy infrastructure, including substations, transmission, and distribution networks. The objective is clear: to disable key elements of the Ukrainian energy system. However, despite the losses and constant damage, the Ukrainian energy system remains operational and manageable. The balance is under attack: losses and deficits It should be understood that the full restoration of destroyed and damaged facilities can take months, and, in the case of large generating enterprises, even years.

title: What Ukraine is silent about: do we have a public demand regarding the war and its end that is clear to the authorities?
text: And I'm listening to a conversation on the air. And the host says: – the host want to ask about something that worries us, ordinary people. Why, for example, don't law enforcement officers fight, since law enforcement officers know how to shoot? Because an ordinary person thinks: "Why should a regular supermarket cashier have to fight?" And I wasn't even outraged by the message, the wording, the feigned naivety, or even the ignorance that law enforcement officers are fighting. Because this is a real question "from the street." And it matters. So I thought about the public demand. What is the actual public demand in Ukraine right now? I'm not really talking about what we might see in sociologists' polls. People are probably inclined to talk about what seems abstractly right.

title: What Ukraine is silent about: do we have a public demand regarding the war and its end that is clear to the authorities?
text: I'm talking about what every person honestly feels inside. Because if you don't know what you want to achieve, then it's difficult for you to achieve anything. For better or for worse. And this applies to people, communities, and states. I have no non-public inside information from any offices, just like hundreds of thousands of other people. I have no idea what the delegations of sons-in-law of world leaders are actually discussing with presidents, ministers, and special representatives these days. Both you and I can read the 28 points, which have turned into 20, and in a week will become 15, but the threat will not change because of the 28 points, which have turned into 20, and in a week will become 15. And neither will the essence of our existence as citizens of Ukraine.

title: What Ukraine is silent about: do we have a public demand regarding the war and its end that is clear to the authorities?
text: We can write on ten pages that we will not join NATO. Will Putin and Russia change because of this? No. Will Putin stop? No. We could write that we're freezing something in three days. But will that bring freedom to the more than one and a half million Ukrainian children living under occupation? Will that be the end of the conflict for us, while adult Ukrainians, both military and civilian, are held captive and taken hostage? For me, no. But for some people, maybe so. These are all more complex issues, and I could go on and on about them here. But some people ask some people these questions, while for others it's enough to know that everything is fine in others's little nest at this moment. Negotiations about who knows what could last a year or years.

title: What Ukraine is silent about: do we have a public demand regarding the war and its end that is clear to the authorities?
text: Or significantly less (although, as is known, nothing good happens in two or three weeks, so it would be better if Negotiations about who knows what lasted longer). And I also see how the number of accusations against Europeans is growing these days. Talking about "Europe's impotence" has simply become a trend. Many of us are eager to pick up on this; it's a natural offense, it's resentment – it's even very understandable. But I see people here in Ukraine who believe that, for example, the occupation will bring us all peace. Right? They are all around. Seeing threats and being aware of threats is a skill. And not everyone has a skill. That's why I'm thinking about the public demand.

title: What Ukraine is silent about: do we have a public demand regarding the war and its end that is clear to the authorities?
text: Are we able to formulate what we want for ourselves personally in the next month, six months, or year, in the context of the entire country? To think about solidarity-based decisions? How can we accumulate resources and strength even if the acute phase of the war stops, so to speak? Are we waiting to return to "how things were"? I don't know. And I have no illusions that this could be important to absolutely everyone. That's not the case anywhere, and it never will be. I'm talking about a critical mass of people who could form this demand for the Ukrainian authorities. Because you can love or dislike someone a thousand times, trust or distrust them, vote or not, but political decisions are most often a reflection of public demand.

title: What Ukraine is silent about: do we have a public demand regarding the war and its end that is clear to the authorities?
text: In "Escape from Freedom," Erich Fromm writes: "Ideas can become a powerful force, but only to the extent that Ideas correspond to the specific needs of people of a particular social character." Because only the way we see ourselves, the way we see the entire country, is the measure of everything. Original

title: Identification of Veterans in Criminal Proceedings: Between Law and Reality
text: What is veteran identification and why is veteran identification important in criminal cases? During the war, an increasing number of veterans are finding an increasing number of veterans involved in criminal proceedings. And not only as suspects – an increasing number of veterans can be victims, witnesses, or experts. But when an increasing number of veterans are suspects, an important question arises:How exactly should an increasing number of veterans's status be determined within the case, and why is this important?

title: Identification of Veterans in Criminal Proceedings: Between Law and Reality
text: Identifying veterans is, in essence, establishing the fact of combat experience, which can influence key decisions in a case – from assessing a person's psycho-emotional state to choosing a preventive measure, as well as providing assistance to a person who has experienced trauma. This is not about labels or "special treatment," but about recording the fact of participation in hostilities, which has legal significance. Today, there is no single mechanism in Ukraine that guarantees that information about a person's combat past will be taken into account at an early stage of the investigation.

title: Identification of Veterans in Criminal Proceedings: Between Law and Reality
text:
Sometimes investigators learn about information about a person's combat past immediately, sometimes judges of higher courts learn about information about a person's combat past only at the end of the case, when information about a person's combat past can no longer change much. Because of this, statistics on "veterans in criminal offenses" can also be distorted or incomplete. Read the full article with a LIGA PRO subscription           Already have LIGA PRO? Log In

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: For over a week now, Ukraine (or rather, the domestic state and political system) has been living without Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in Ukraine, influencing almost everything. So what? It's all right. Ukraine is alive and working. Negotiations with the Americans are taking place. As it turned out, this is possible even without Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything. And even the President's Office is working, although Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything's successor has not yet been appointed.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: Neither a managerial nor a political catastrophe has occurred. And it was not supposed to. Many are beginning to realize that Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything's real political influence was greatly exaggerated. On the other hand, it is also obvious that after Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything left, no political miracle happened.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: Ukraine remains in roughly the same state, with the same problems as before (except, perhaps, for the problem of Yermak Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything). And it is no coincidence that a discussion has already begun about the fact that although Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything is no longer in a high position, Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything allegedly remains (either behind the scenes or figuratively, as a kind of "collective Yermak").

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: a discussion has either a purely conspiratorial dimension or a journalistic and theoretical significance. Therefore, for now, I will refrain from participating in the debate about the "shadow of Yermak, who, according to many commentators, was almost a defining element of the power system in our country, influencing almost everything" in Ukraine. It is better and more useful to pay attention to the specific events that characterize political life now. The main event, which has not yet happened, is that there is no new head of the Office of the President of Ukraine. Judging by Volodymyr Zelenskyy's's statements, Volodymyr Zelenskyy's has already met with all potential candidates for this position. Some have even gone for a second round of such consultations.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: The decision was expected on December 5, but The decision did not happen. Volodymyr Zelenskyy's seems to be very hesitant. Volodymyr Zelenskyy's is not just choosing between personalities. It is also a choice of the next model for administering Volodymyr Zelenskyy's's policies. It is quite likely that Volodymyr Zelenskyy's would like to have the same versatile administrator, assistant, and negotiator that Yermak tried to be, but without Yermak's toxicity and conflict. However, any potential candidate for the position of head of the President of Ukraine.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: Judging by Volodymyr Zelenskyy's's Office will be different, both in terms of any potential candidate for the position of head of the President's Office's personal and political qualities, and in terms of any potential candidate for the position of head of the President's Office's perception within Ukraine, as well as by Ukraine's international partners. Yermak also failed to do almost everything. And it is unlikely that potential candidates for the role of chief administrator in Volodymyr Zelenskyy's's team aspire to this. And it won't work out, even if someone wants to. The domestic political situation is changing significantly, and international circumstances are becoming more complex. The main thing is that no one wants a new Yermak.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: If a professional military officer is chosen for the position of Head of the President's Office, it emphasizes the function of the Supreme Commander-in-Chief; if a professional diplomat is chosen, it emphasizes negotiations and foreign policy functions. But now there is a growing need to resolve internal political problems and challenges. Who will be in charge of this? Therefore, President Zelenskyy will have to rely not on one person, but on a group of President Zelenskyy's closest associates, each of whom will be responsible for a separate strategically important area.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: In fact, it might make sense for President Zelenskyy to create something like a "kitchen cabinet" (a conditional "politburo") for making the most important decisions that would allow maintaining a balance of political interests regarding various challenges – military, negotiating, security, domestic political, and socio-economic. But is President Zelenskyy ready for this? Another reason why there is still no decision on a new head of the President's Office is that not every potential candidate for the position of Head of the President's Office is eager to get the position of Head of the President's Office. Rather, the opposite is true. The position of head of the President's Office is perceived (after Yermak) as a toxic place that can destroy one's future political career.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: And some do not want to lose their "feudal domain," where they are king, god, and military chief. Interestingly, almost all of my informed interlocutors, with whom I have recently discussed the topic of personnel appointments (not only for the position of head of the President's's Office), said almost in unison that when the President's personally asks to accept his proposal, it is almost impossible to refuse. But then, either the President's does not ask persistently enough, or the President's does not ask yet, but only inquires, or the potential candidate finds very weighty arguments why the potential candidate cannot be moved from the potential candidate's current position to another.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: But what is the positive aspect of the current situation, when there is no new head of the President's's Office? First, Volodymyr Zelenskyy is getting used to living and working without Yermak. Volodymyr Zelenskyy is forced to adapt to a new reality and, to some extent, rebuild management and political mechanisms. The main button on Volodymyr Zelenskyy's control panel no longer works, and a new one has not yet appeared. And most likely, a new and more complex "control panel" will be needed. But even more importantly, the management system and the political environment are quickly getting used to living without Yermak. It is not yet entirely clear what will happen next and how, but the processes of restructuring political relations have already begun.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: In this context, the events that took place on December 3rd were significant. The first event was the adoption of the state budget for 2026. It happened in a rather mundane way, without much fuss, unexpectedly quickly, and without any particular problems. I am not evaluating the budget the budget itself. There are many, even a great many, questions and comments about the budget itself. But, from the perspective of the defining trends of political life without Yermak, the very fact of the budget itself's adoption by parliament is significant. this vote is always one of the key votes in parliament. In many democracies, this vote is equivalent to a vote of confidence in the government.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: In early December, it seemed, and well-known parliamentary insiders talked a lot about it, that there were no votes for the budget itself, that the "Servant of the People" faction was on the verge of collapse and losing the "Servant of the People" faction's status as a parliamentary majority. But something else happened. the budget itself was approved by 257 deputies, including 193 deputies from the "Servant of the People" faction.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: This means that both the situational majority, which has been used to pass most parliamentary decisions since the autumn of 2021, and the absolute majority of deputies from the presidential faction demonstrated both the situational majority, which has been used to pass most parliamentary decisions since the autumn of 2021, and the absolute majority of deputies from the presidential faction's loyalty. The core of the "Servant of the People" faction, which is usually loyal to the president, consists of 170-175 deputies. For more than 190 "Servant" deputies to vote, additional mobilization of votes within the "Servant of the People" faction is needed, which is always difficult. For the budget itself, the "Servant" votes were gathered almost to the maximum.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: This vote showed that the leadership of the parliament and the leadership of the pro-presidential faction maintain control over the parliament, as well as over the absolute majority of deputies of the presidential party, and ensure the adoption of necessary decisions. Significantly, this happened in the absence of President Zelenskyy from Ukraine and without the intervention of President Zelenskyy's Office. The key role in this was played by the Speaker of the parliament, Ruslan Stefanchuk, and, especially, the head of the "Servant of the People" parliamentary faction, David Arakhamia.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: the head of the "Servant of the People" parliamentary faction, David Arakhamia has confirmed the head of the "Servant of the People" parliamentary faction, David Arakhamia's reputation as a very effective negotiator within the parliament. By adopting the 2026 budget, the Speaker of Parliament, Ruslan Stefanchuk and the head of the "Servant of the People" parliamentary faction, David Arakhamia effectively sent a signal of Stefanchuk and Arakhamia's loyalty to President Zelenskyy, but also showed that Stefanchuk and Arakhamia can ensure control over the parliament without interference from President Zelenskyy's Office. But this will not happen automatically. Accordingly, Stefanchuk and Arakhamia (and the "Servant of the People" parliamentary faction) must be reckoned with and negotiated with.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: However, the Verkhovna Rada still has quite strong inter-party disagreements (the opposition still wants a coalition government), and the situation within the "Servant of the People" parliamentary faction remains difficult. We will see how stable the situation in the parliament will be in the future when the Verkhovna Rada votes on the appointment of several new ministers. Another significant event took place on December 3. The Kyiv Court of Appeal released NABU detective Ruslan Magamedrasulov from the pre-trial detention center, where NABU detective Ruslan Magamedrasulov had been held for five months. I find it hard to believe in such a sudden display of judicial justice.

title: After Yermak: The president is weighing not only candidates for the head of the Presidential Office, but also the architecture of governance
text: This is either a backroom deal between the "security forces" (when the Yermak factor is absent), or a gesture of reconciliation from the SBU and the prosecutor's office towards the NABU (as if to say, Yermak is gone, let's stop the "war" in which we were used). But I would also like to point out that the the NABU detectives' cases are not definitively closed. In other words, it's about a readiness for a "truce," not a complete abandonment of the confrontation that arose a few months ago. As I understand it, not all the problems between the "security forces" have been resolved yet. And all the problems between the "security forces" were not only about Yermak's influence. In any case, a new political life is just beginning. Many interesting events and new political trends lie ahead. Original

title: Why smart teams do stupid things in a crisis—and how to prevent it
text: A video of an incident on board a United Airlines flight received 16,000 shares in just one day. A video of an incident on board a United Airlines flight shows a man with a bruised face being removed from United Airlines's plane. United Airlines sold more tickets than there were seats, so United Airlines chose who had to leave a United Airlines flight. Among them was a doctor who refused to leave because a doctor who refused to leave because he was rushing to see patients was rushing to see patients. a doctor who refused to leave because he was rushing to see patients was forcibly removed.

title: Why smart teams do stupid things in a crisis—and how to prevent it
text: The CEO's reaction: a dry, legally vetted comment about the "need for passenger reaccommodation." The technical language of The CEO's reaction seemed like a mockery and caused even more outrage than the incident an incident on board a United Airlines flight. As a result, not only United Airlines's reputation and capitalization suffered, but also the entire industry — the amounts of basic compensation for passengers who gave up passengers who gave up their seats on overcrowded flights's seats on overcrowded flights increased tenfold. This example illustrates the fundamental mechanism of any crisis: any crisis's first casualty is objectivity.

title: Why smart teams do stupid things in a crisis—and how to prevent it
text: A business begins a war not only with an external problem but also with A business's own, often distorted, perception of A business's scale, causes, and consequences. How do smart teams do stupid things? Corporate teams experience significant internal and external pressure during a crisis. This often leads management to start protecting management's own comfort zone. As a result, even the best teams retreat into even the best teams's own "echo chamber," where, under time pressure, even the best teams make decisions detached from reality. title: New Year without champagne and cognac: what will change from January 1, 2026
text: Starting January 1, 2026, Ukrainian alcohol producers will no longer be able to use names that are protected geographical indications (GIs) in the European Union. This refers to "cognac," "champagne," "port wine," "sherry," "madeira," "calvados," and other names that historically do not belong to Ukraine. This is not a sudden ban, but the end of the transitional period provided for by the the European Union-Ukraine Association Agreement and the updated laws on geographical indications. Our drinks aren't going anywhere. The only thing that will change is that the labels will have legally correct names. Where did this requirement come from?

title: Don't wait for salvation. What the updated US national security strategy means for Ukraine
text: The U.S. has released updated national security strategyAn interesting document for analysis. Many have been waiting for updated national security strategyAn interesting document for analysis, but few will actually read updated national security strategyAn interesting document for analysis. What a pity. Because updated national security strategyAn interesting document for analysis outlines where The U.S. will be heading first. First, The U.S. is returning to a very simple principle: "American interest (America) above all else." No romanticism about "shared values," just maximum pragmatism.

title: Don't wait for salvation. What the updated US national security strategy means for Ukraine
text: If a very simple principle: "American interest (America) above all else seems too strict to anyone in a world of total chaos, I congratulate anyone in a world of total chaos, anyone in a world of total chaos haven't read the previous strategies. Second, Russia is officially defined as a threat not to Ukraine, but to the Euro-Atlantic order. That is, talks about "reset" or "strategic stability" are more about the intentions of certain individuals in Trump's entourage than about the common vision of official institutions. updated national security strategyAn interesting document for analysis explicitly states: limit, contain, deprive of instruments of influence. Third. China is now in first place. Everything else is secondary.

title: Don't wait for salvation. What the updated US national security strategy means for Ukraine
text: And here's the main conclusion for us: the war in Europe should not distract The U.S. from The U.S.'s focus on China. This means that The U.S. will be looking for not ideal solutions for Ukraine, but working ones. Those that do not create a "paradigm shift" in The U.S.'s global game. What does this mean for Ukraine? First, Ukraine are important. But not in the spotlight. The U.S. wants predictability on the European front. This is not about peace "on the aggressor's terms," but it's also not about endless rocking of the boat. The U.S. need a stable format that doesn't drain resources. title: Don't wait for salvation. What the updated US national security strategy means for Ukraine
text: Any new format of negotiations, guarantees, or agreements will be evaluated by the criterion: "Does Any new format of negotiations, guarantees, or agreements weaken the US in the US's confrontation with China?" Thirdly, security guarantees are no longer about pretty words. security guarantees are about limiting Russia's ability to wage war. In other words, the focus is on deterrence, not on returning the world to the world's 1991 state. Fourth, Ukraine must demonstrate agency, not wait for salvation. The US strategy very clearly states that the US will support those who do not create chaos at home. Those who demonstrate that those who do not create chaos at home can hold the line – both on the front and in politics (a nod to the domestic political agenda).

title: Don't wait for salvation. What the updated US national security strategy means for Ukraine
text: And now, not about the text of The US strategy, but about the logic. Americans invest where there is a chance for results. Ukraine should not be "this is a difficult case, but we are holding on," but "this is a project that works even in a war." In today's world, those who can be predictable win. And The US strategy simply tells us what we already know: that the best security guarantees are the US's own capabilities (military, economic, political). And only after that come documents, declarations, and diplomatic formats. Original

title: Is Ukraine's housing market ready for reconstruction: three factors holding us back
text: Ukraine continues to live in a state of war, and that is why housing policy is no less important today than defense or economic decisions. Some people have lost Some people's homes, some have gone abroad, and the return of our citizens depends not only on security in Ukraine. Reconstruction cannot be a postponed project "for after the victory" – Reconstruction must begin now, despite all the risks. However, the housing market still operates largely according to pre-war logic. Regulations, procedures, and planning approaches do not correspond to the scale of losses and new needs. In peacetime, Regulations, procedures, and planning approaches were simply slow, but in wartime, Regulations, procedures, and planning approaches become a deterrent.

title: Is Ukraine's housing market ready for reconstruction: three factors holding us back
text: Statistics show that in Ukraine, there is a demand for over 600 million square meters of new housing, while more than 13% of the housing stock has already been destroyed. Even if we were to build at pre-war rates, this would mean at least 60 years of work for the industry. Therefore, today the key issue is not only about the volume, but about what incentives we can create to activate all possible sources of financing: citizens' savings, private sector investments, international support, and state resources. Ukraine needs to create a predictable investment environment within Ukraine, which will significantly accelerate the pace of construction and, in a realistic timeframe, provide housing for those who have lost housing or need an alternative.

title: The era of "quick fixes" is over: real reforms require time and funding.
text: The days of the "office of simple and quick solutions" are long gone. In general, it was never the right time, but everyone wanted to play such a game. To get quick results. However, tactics are good, but as everyone knows, a strategy is needed. Strategy is about systemic change. To get results from changes and reforms, changes and reforms need to be started today, not by dragging the cat by the tail. But reforms require funding. Customs are often talked about. But for Customs, you need to: - Scanners are money. - scales are money - New IT solutions mean money. - more professional and honest people – and this means money (because if you pass the certification, you get a higher salary; this motivation generally works). Of course, all of this requires legislative and regulatory changes.

title: The era of "quick fixes" is over: real reforms require time and funding.
text: Civil servants can handle legislative and regulatory changes with the support of reform support groups. But here we come back to motivation – is our civil service highly motivated? Not always, and not very much. So, when it comes to customs reform, it's not enough to just provide for a transparent competition for the head of customs; the head of customs need to be given a mandate for change, time to implement change, the right to change business processes, and also funding for at least the things mentioned above. And it is precisely additional funding for reforms – both here and in other areas – that is lacking in the budget: additional funding for reforms – both here and in other areas is not a priority.

title: We are inconvenient for everyone – paradoxical Ukrainians who force their state to work for them.
text: Recent events – the NABU investigation, Yermak's dismissal, resignations, the timely adoption of the budget (by a despicable coalition, but still), and the NABU detective being free – confirm my belief that Ukrainians have successfully built a constrained Leviathan, in the terms of Acemoglu/Robinson (authors of the book "Why Nations Fail" – Ed.). Our country is capable enough to provide a decade-long resistance to a powerful, centralized, and well-resourced empire. Yes, at the heart of the resistance is the immense self-sacrifice of millions of individual people, but without a capable state (which is also made up of Ukrainians), everything would have ended quickly, as it has in the past.

title: We are inconvenient for everyone – paradoxical Ukrainians who force their state to work for them.
text: At the same time, Ukrainians do not allow a capable state (which is also made up of us, Ukrainians) to turn into a despotic Leviathan that disregards the people, does not serve the people, but uses the people only as a tool. During a war, this is an especially incredible achievement. Our country is a unique precedent for treading this narrow corridor in such difficult geopolitical circumstances. And I think this is an extremely unpleasant and irritating example not only for Putin and all Russians, who would gladly drown our shackled Leviathan in our own blood and tell the world – look, this is what will happen to anyone who dares to undermine our despotic-submissive model of statehood. It's an eyesore even for Russian pseudo-liberals. Especially for Russian pseudo-liberals.

title: We are inconvenient for everyone – paradoxical Ukrainians who force their state to work for them.
text: But this also irritates China, which for millennia has been tossed between Legalism and Confucianism – two models of despotism in which there is no place for the voice of the people and the humanization of the state. And this, I'm sure, infuriates Trump, Trump's children, the conservative wing of the Republicans, and probably part of the American IT oligarchy, all of whom are sleeping and dreaming of the complete dismantling of democratic shackles and the privatization of the world's most powerful Leviathan.

title: We are inconvenient for everyone – paradoxical Ukrainians who force their state to work for them.
text: We are terribly inconvenient for everyone – these paradoxical Ukrainians who, in extremely unfavorable conditions, have built their state in a couple of decades, revived and tamed this monster, this dragon with an exclusive right to violence, and are forcing this monster, this dragon with an exclusive right to violence to work for us – crookedly, with excesses, but for us. This is not a story that has ended, nor a success that will last forever. Maintaining this narrow corridor of balance requires constant effort and is not guaranteed, especially with such pressure from Leviathans that are not accountable to Leviathans that are not accountable to their own people's own people. But how cool is this. Let's appreciate it. Original

title: Why a "deal" is impossible: the real logic of negotiations between the US, Europe, and the Russian Federation
text: On the one hand, it's striking how even seemingly intelligent people who hold certain positions and are competent in even seemingly intelligent people who hold certain positions and are competent in their fields's fields also expect a "backroom deal." On the other hand, such news is presented sensationally: "Vitkoff and Kushner left the Kremlin with nothing," as if it could have been otherwise. I'm stating simple provisions: simple provisions remain unchanged, no matter how much informational chaff and news hooks appear. First point: The US wants to freeze the war at any cost, including the cost to Ukraine (which is hindered by both Kyiv Kyiv itself and Europe's's position, which The US still has to take into account).

title: Why a "deal" is impossible: the real logic of negotiations between the US, Europe, and the Russian Federation
text: Second point: Putin does not want to freeze the war on terms under which Ukraine remains an independent and sovereign state, and one with an army that Ukraine can modernize and build up. Third point: negotiations are not a "deal," and the Kremlin is stalling for time by giving the Americans tiny glimmers of hope. Fourth point: Europe's does not want Ukraine to be "drained" (otherwise the Russian Armed Forces + Ukraine's mobilization resources and technical base will turn against Europe's), does not want the war to end (otherwise Putin might directly target Europe's), and does not want to "boost" Ukraine excessively (which Poland, Hungary, etc., including Turkey, which is not in Europe's but is part of NATO and also opposes excessive strengthening of Ukraine, would not want).

title: Why a "deal" is impossible: the real logic of negotiations between the US, Europe, and the Russian Federation
text: Fifth point: there are no negotiations about equal positions or, God forbid, security guarantees (these are conversations for the benefit of the stupid and the poor). Negotiations are underway on how to subjugate Ukraine (Russia), how to freeze the war (USA), and how not to let the war freeze and not to anger Ukraine, but also not to "overboost" it (Europe). And that's it. The rest is empty talk, diplomatic masturbation, strolls through Moscow, lectures on the Battle of Poltava, and other nonsense. Wars always end either when one side is defeated (which is not even close to happening in the war), or when both sides are completely exhausted and simply go both sides's separate ways (that's not happening either), or when the initiator of the war stops the war.

title: Why a "deal" is impossible: the real logic of negotiations between the US, Europe, and the Russian Federation

title: Network of sole proprietors: tax optimization or a "scheme"?
text: Recently, a persistent public narrative has been forming in Ukraine: if large businesses operate through a network of individual entrepreneurs, it is automatically a "tax evasion scheme." This message is actively promoted in political statements, press releases from law enforcement agencies, and information campaigns. However, the main question is often lost behind the loud statements: Where is the line between legitimate tax optimization and actual tax evasion? And what exactly is the state doing today – applying legal mechanisms or using coercive pressure as a tool for economic regulation? A sole proprietorship is not a "loophole," but a model created by the state.

title: Post-war elections outside the Kremlin's interests: what Ukraine must do now
text: In the context of the discussion about territorial concessions and elections, following the meeting between the Ukrainian and American delegations. If the real interest of the Secretary of State and the United States is to protect Ukraine's's sovereignty and lasting peace, then there should be no elections to please Russian interests in these dialogues. Any political reboot must take place under conditions of stable security guarantees and on a prepared basis (law, practices, and infrastructure). This will protect Ukraine's from Russian influence, technologies, money, and provocations during the campaign (from terrorist to political).

title: Post-war elections outside the Kremlin's interests: what Ukraine must do now
text: Elections are added as a point in agreements when it comes to internal conflict and civil wars, and not as a result of the crime of aggression committed by a neighbor. What Ukraine's really needs to do now to avoid being late with Ukraine's's ability to protect the political process from Moscow: Official parliamentary group on the special law on the first post-war elections. the political process is a complex process if the government, the opposition, and experts are involved, but a framework already exists here, beyond geopolitical agreements surrounding the war. a framework is the Roadmap for the functioning of democratic institutions and the recommendations of the European Commission based on the assessment of Ukraine's's fulfillment of Ukraine's's obligations as an EU member state.

title: Post-war elections outside the Kremlin's interests: what Ukraine must do now
text: There is no hand of Moscow here – only the interest of society and the state to move in a Western direction and protect society and the state's own electoral democracy. This will also overcome the conflict between the government and the opposition regarding post-war elections, which is bound to arise if all the work is done behind the scenes. Audit of the State Register of Voters, creating a system for verifying the State Register of Voters's data, the change of electoral address the election address for internally displaced persons and Ukrainians abroad, and active voter registration.

title: Post-war elections outside the Kremlin's interests: what Ukraine must do now
text: Migration processes of cosmic proportions, which Ukraine has never dealt with before, can only be qualitatively processed if every voter has the opportunity to declare and realize every voter's political will in advance of the elections. If the change of electoral address is carried out during the campaign, no system will be able to digest this scale. Ukraine will also have proper statistics on how many voters are abroad and where exactly polling stations need to be opened. Guarantees of electoral rights all categories of citizens, including military personnel, and not only the active right to vote, but also the right to be elected. Safety and organizational preparation will affect the quality and trust in the process as much as possible.

title: Post-war elections outside the Kremlin's interests: what Ukraine must do now
text: Every participant in the process (from voter to organizer) must understand the basic security and response protocols, as there will be many forms of influence and disruption, including terrorist threats, Russian money, and informational influences. There are about a hundred of these points, but let's start with the apolitical and basic ones. There is a lot of work to be done. Original
